Happening on the same day in Amsterdam:

Tech Lead Conf - a conference for hands-on engineering leaders.

– Cambridge Dictionary

Nation — a large group of people who share the same language, traditions, and history

JSNation is a 2-day 2-track event focusing on full-stack engineering. Discover the future of the Web development ecosystem and get connected to its stellar crowd!

— JSNation & React Summit were two of the best organized events I have ever come across. The attention to detail, quality of curation and how memorable you made it for attendees and speakers was great. Thanks for a terrific show!
Addy Osmani
Chrome DevTools Tech Lead, Google

Learn more

Deep Dives

Full-Stack Development & Architecture

Following increasing demand for Full-stack Software Engineering, we'll dive deep into modern practices applicable to JS and Web Developers Architecture, Data, Security, DevOps of AI and Full-stack devs.
To be covered in:
  • Workshop: DevOps for Front-end Developers: From Local Code to Production
  • Talk: Designing a Migration to Micro-Frontends
  • Talk: Walking the Netflix Paved Road (Bumps Included): Web Framework, Hawkins Design System
  • Talk: Rewrite or Refactor? How to Safely Move Legacy Apps to Modern Frameworks
  • Talk: Auth Under Attack Catching JavaScript Auth Failures in Production Fast
  • Talk: Black Friday: Would You Choose the Right Performance Test?
Master this Deep Dive with Multipass

AI Assisted Coding

Learn how to navigate rapidly evolving evolution of AI developer tooling. See how teams supercharge code reviews, code analysis and observability; learn how to “babysit“ background agents and 10X your output.
To be covered in:
  • Workshop: Advanced Claude Code — Production Workflows, Subagents, and Autonomous Execution
  • Workshop: Building Full-Stack Apps with Cursor
  • Workshop: Let’s Teach Neo Kung-Fu: Building an Agentic Skill with MCP Tools
  • Talk: Templates and Components for Claude Code: The Future of AI Coding Workflows
  • Talk: Autonomous AI Agents in Action With the Ralph Wiggum Method
  • Talk: MemLab: Automating Memory Leak Detection and Heap Analysis
  • Talk: LLM Powered Migration of UI Component Libraries
  • Talk: From 8 Years to 6 Months: Orchestrating AI Agents for Large-Scale Refactoring
Master this Deep Dive with Multipass

AI Engineering

What skills are needed to implement AI strategies and succeed as a developer in the AI era?
To be covered in:
  • Workshop: Operating Agent-Based Systems - Overview, Configure, Run, Orchestrate, Monitor
  • Workshop: Voice Agent Bootcamp: Build Production-Ready Agents from Scratch
  • Workshop: Building AI-Powered Apps with TanStack AI - From Setup to Chat Tools
  • Talk: Click. Ship. Done. AI Agents on Cloudflare

Growing to Senior & TechLead

Master the essential hard and soft skills needed to grow from a Software Engineer into a Tech Lead or Staff Engineer. Learn directly from distinguished engineers how to accelerate your career.
To be covered in:
  • Talk: 400 Tech Leads. Same Problems. None of Them Technical
  • Talk: Building a JavaScript Engine in Rust: Lessons Learned
Master this Deep Dive with Multipass
This year, authors, core teams, and community experts will share their insights on:
  • Claude Code
  • OpenClaw
  • Cursor
  • TanStack AI
  • vibe-kanban
  • TypeScript
  • Vite
  • Docker
  • Turbopack
  • Boa.js
  • Node.js

Features

Live legends & Rising stars

Learn from the ones who write the history of the JS nation

Networking

Meet new friends & new opportunities, join speaker & discussion rooms

City tours

Explore Amsterdam with new friends during our boat and walking tours

Eat, Sip & Stay Fueled

Start strong at the pancake station and keep the good vibes going till the final talk — meals, drinks, and sweet bites all day

Food Truck Festival Lunch

Juicy burgers, fresh bowls, and more

The biggest JS party worldwide

JS Open Source Awards

It's our mission to support OS projects and celebrate them annually at the OS Awards. As an open foundation, we are looking forward to collaborating with like-minded individuals and businesses to help us propagate the OS culture even further

Line-up

The State of AI for Web Development Sacha Greif
Chunking Tobias Koppers

MCP Apps – the Next Web Liad Yosef
FullStack Monitoring with Open Telemetry: End-to-End Observability for Modern Applications Erick Wendel
Designing a Migration to Micro-Frontends Luca Mezzalira
Building a JavaScript Engine in Rust: Lessons From Boa Jason Williams

Autonomous AI Agents in Action With the Ralph Wiggum Method Eddy Vinck
Orchestrating Content Workflows at Netflix Scale Pratyusha Singaraju
Simpler Desktop Apps With Deno Leo Kettmeir

Aleksei Petrov
QuantFlow Studio, Montenegro
Workshop: Advanced Claude Code — Production Workflows, Subagents, and Autonomous Execution

Aleksei Petrov is a CTO and the co-founder of QuantFlow Studio. Anthropic Claude Community Ambassador. Uses Claude Code daily to ship production software. Author of Pilot – a top rated open-source autonomous coding agent. Navigator, a Claude Code plugin for context engineering. Based in Podgorica, Montenegro.

Full Info
web standards
Liad Yosef
MCP Apps, Israel
Talk: MCP Apps – the Next Web

Liad Yosef is a seasoned AI lead and software architect. He is the co-builder of MCP-UI, the co-author and maintainer of MCP Apps on the MCP Steering Committee, and a co-creator of GitMCP. Previously AI Lead in Shopify's CEO office, leading agentic interfaces, and currently the co-founder and CTO building the future of human-agentic interfaces at Era Labs. Liad is a web enthusiast two decades, passionate about crafting developer-first experiences. When he isn't defining open-source standards for the agentic web, he writes poetry and moonlights as an analog astronaut for the European Space Agency.

Full Info
js engine
Jason Williams
Bloomberg LP, UK
Talk: Building a JavaScript Engine in Rust: Lessons From Boa

Jason Williams is a Senior Software Engineer at Bloomberg LP in London, UK, focusing on performance. His work in performance includes collaborating with the W3C Web Performance Working Group to bring Container Timing to the web, a more accurate improvement over Largest Contentful Paint. He has also collaborated on V8 improvements, bringing heat snapshot times down from 10 minutes to 6 seconds.

In the open-source community, he is the original author and developer of Boa, an open-source JavaScript engine written in Rust.

He is heavily involved in the evolution of ECMAScript, serving as an author on the Stage 4 proposal for Promise.allSettled, which ensures all promises settle before returning results. He is also a champion for the Stage 3 Temporal proposal (for which Boa’s temporal_rs the implementation used in Chrome), which aims to provide standard objects and functions for working with dates and times in ECMAScript.

Full Info
backend

architecture
Pratyusha Singaraju
Netflix, USA
Talk: Orchestrating Content Workflows at Netflix Scale

Pratyusha Singaraju is a Senior Software Engineer at Netflix Inc., where she architects and builds sophisticated backend services that power the content understanding infrastructure behind Netflix's search, collections, and ratings systems. Her work directly impacts how millions of users discover and engage with content across the platform. Prior to Netflix, Pratyusha was part of Microsoft's data team for Bing, where she worked on knowledge graph initiatives spanning diverse public domains, including food, nutrition, politics, events, and building an enterprise knowledge graph.

Maurice de Beijer
Independent Software Consultant and Trainer, Netherlands
Workshop: Building Fullstack Apps With Cursor

Maurice de Beijer is an independent software consultant and trainer. He specializes in TypeScript, ECMAScript, React and Svelte. His work includes popular collaboration software as well as a large, global, safety application for the oil and gas industry. He has a preference for working with startups and smaller, more dynamic companies. Maurice is also active in the open source community. He teaches ECMAScript, TypeScript, React, Cypress, Playwright and RxJS courses. Since 2005, he has received Microsoft’s Yearly Most Valuable Professional Award. Further, Maurice is active in the Dutch dotNed user group and helps organize its meetings.

devops
Karthick Shanmugam
Sixt SE, Germany, Munich
Talk: Scaling JavaScript Monorepos at Enterprise Level: Lessons From 200+ Packages

I'm Karthick, a Staff Web Platform Developer at SIXT, currently focused on making AI coding agents safe and productive inside large React codebases — without letting them erode the architecture boundaries that make them work. Beyond AI, I spend my time building JavaScript platforms, refining workspace-driven architectures, and designing the infrastructure layers that tie everything together. From Nx/pnpm monorepos to multi-instance microfrontends, I focus on the patterns, boundaries, and shared capabilities that help application teams scale without friction. I love diving into how large JS systems behave in the real world and sharing practical lessons that simplify them.

Talk: A Brief History of Code Review (And What's Next)

Santosh is a Principal Developer Advocate at CodeRabbit, a GDE for Angular, GitHub Star, Nx Champion and Microsoft MVP. He loves contributing to Angular and its ecosystem and loves monorepos.

Full Info
Bernie Sumption
AG Grid, UK
Talk: Debugging Performance With AI

Bernie is an engineer at AG Grid specialising in theming. "The other engineers make it work fast and well, I make it look pretty" he likes to say. Outside work he goes hiking, plays with his kids, and once made a tweeting cat flap that has 10x more social media followers than he does.

Full Info
Max Drake
tldraw, UK
Talk: Agents on the Canvas With tldraw

Max Drake is a product engineer at tldraw, a London-based startup building an infinite canvas component for the web. The company is known for its tldraw SDK as well as for many viral AI demos including Make Real and tldraw computer. Previously, she was a design researcher working on interacting with LLMs on a programmable canvas.

Full Info
Steven Hao
Cognition, USA
Talk: Taste in Software Development

Steven Hao is the co-founder and Chief Technology Officer of Cognition, the AI lab behind Devin, the autonomous AI software engineer. He helped shape Devin from an early research project into a product now deployed across some of the world's largest enterprises. Before Cognition, Steven was a gold medalist for the United States at the International Olympiad in Informatics and studied Computer Science and Mathematics at MIT.

Our MCs

Full Info
CJ Reynolds
Syntax.fm / Sentry, USA
Senior Creator @ Syntax.fm / Sentry
Full Info
Scott Tolinski
Co-host of Syntax.fm, USA

I own and make video tutorials teaching web development for Level Up Tutorials, releasing a new premium series every month.

I also co-host Syntax.fm with Wes Bos. Syntax is a popular, light hearted web development podcast that teaches full stack topics while staying fun.

I enjoy breaking also known as breakdancing, and have been dancing for over 15 years doing shows for professional NFL and NBA teams. I'm inspired by a hot cup of green tea, excellent music, and Shaw Bros. kung fu movies.

Full Info
Jessie Auguste
CybSafe, UK

Jessie Auguste is an award-winning Software Engineer at CybSafe and co-founder of Glowing in Tech, a community platform empowering underrepresented voices in technology. As a sought-after speaker and conference host, she's delivered talks globally for LeadDev, codebar, Docker, and conferences including React Miami and DevConf South Africa. She's currently completing her Msc in Computer Science at the University of Bath.

Full Info
Karl Horky
Founder @ upleveled.io, Netherlands

As technical founder at upleveled.io, I design and teach our full stack and AI programs, helping beginners and experienced engineers take the next step in their technical growth.

With AI increasingly reshaping technical work, fundamental skills and comprehensive, well-rounded knowledge matter even more. In this context, I focus on designing broad and accessible curricula, mentoring learners at different stages and contributing widely to open source along the way.

Schedule

Program committee

Full Info
Konstantinos Leimonis
monday.com

Konstantinos is as a Software Tech Lead at monday.com on the Client Foundations team, building developer tools and enabling teams with a microfrontend architecture. When he’s not architecting scalable frontend solutions or optimising developer workflows, he’s sharing microfrontend best practices, contributing to the developer tooling ecosystem, and helping teams navigate the complexities of distributed frontend architectures.

Full Info
Valerii Iatsko
Google, Switzerland

With 15+ years spanning distributed systems, real-time data infrastructure, and high-performance UIs across Google, Microsoft, Spotify, and Booking.com, Valerii is passionate about developer experience, low-latency systems, and bridging the gap between complex backend architectures and intuitive user-facing products.

Full Info
Sofia Larsson
IKEA, Sweden

Sofia leads end-to-end product teams building services and customer experiences in a distributed architecture. She cares deeply about creating environments where engineers can do their best work. Outside of work, she co-hosts Developers!, one of Sweden’s most popular software podcasts.

Full Info
Joran Quinten
Fractional Developer Lead, Bonaire, Kralendijk

Jorans passion involves getting people to love technology and getting technology to play nice. He works as a freelance interaction developer with ♡ for web, tech, science & tinkering with stuff.

Published author and conference speaker.

Full Info
Niklas Abrahamsson
Original Creation

Fullstack javascript developer focused on React / Node.js. Always up for a good challenge and learning new stuff. Spends a lot of time with Remix and on the padel court. Organizer of Remix Sweden meetup group.

Full Info
Sergey Berezhnoy
Web Developer

Sergey is a web developer actively engaged in the development of technologies and tools for creating websites. One of the two co-authors of the BEM methodology. Currently he responsible for developer relations, hiring and training in his company.

Full Info
Shivay Lamba
TensorFlowJS Working Group Lead

Shivay Lamba is a software developer specializing in DevOps, Machine Learning and Full Stack Development.

He is an Open Source Enthusiast and has been part of various programs like Google Code In and Google Summer of Code as a Mentor and has also been a MLH Fellow. He is actively involved in community work as well. He is a TensorflowJS SIG member, Mentor in OpenMined and CNCF Service Mesh Community, SODA Foundation and has given talks at various conferences like Github Satellite, Voice Global, Fossasia Tech Summit, TensorflowJS Show & Tell.

Full Info
Anton Nemtsev
Datasnipper
  • 24 years of frontend development. I did see Browser Wars. And mammoths.
  • Independent developer for 16 years. Sold out to corporations.
  • Jack of all trades, master of none. More of the last part.
  • Founder and chief-editor of the Frontender Magazine. Failed miserably.
  • Speaker at international and local conferences. The further the local.
  • UA Web Challenge expert. Ex.
  • Head of the frontend department of the Ukraine online university "Projector". Ex.
  • Magical talking bear prostitute.

Summertime
JS festival

JSNation Conference will gather 1500 JS developers at Kromhouthal. Our venue is a former ship engine factory, and we’re happy to dedicate it to the top JavaScript engine authors and engineers.

Gedempt Hamerkanaal 231

Tech Discussions

Join discussions focusing on specific technologies. Hang out with people who are on the same page.

Discussion rooms on June 11 will be held in a hybrid and in-person formats, while on June 15, fully in a remote format.

01

Trusting AI Systems: How Much Is Too Much?

Jan Peer Stöcklmair, Karthick Shanmugam, Sohan Maheshwar, Sylwia Laskowska, Oleh Koren

Trusting AI Systems: How Much Is Too Much?
02

The New Senior Engineer: Builder, Reviewer, or Orchestrator?

Jan Peer Stöcklmair, Misha Kazakov, Sylwia Laskowska, Frédéric Barthelet

The New Senior Engineer: Builder, Reviewer, or Orchestrator?
03

What Does “Software Engineer” Mean Now?

Alexander Lichter, Luca Mezzalira, Sacha Greif, Karthick Shanmugam, Misha Kazakov, Noah Yamamoto

What Does “Software Engineer” Mean Now?
04

Are We Moving Beyond Code — or Just Writing It Differently?

Tobias Koppers, Alexander Lichter, Luca Mezzalira, Joyee Cheung, Paolo Ricciuti, Sohan Maheshwar, Noah Yamamoto

Are We Moving Beyond Code — or Just Writing It Differently?

The biggest JS party worldwide

Who says that JavaScript is only for serious business? You can actually power an entire party by JS, and JSNation will show you how.

Amsterdam is known for its nightlife and all our attendees get an invitation to join in. If you get full access, you'll get to see various kinds of performances made with JS at the biggest JS party worldwide on June 12.

Aside from that, the extended program for in-person attendees will also offer a karaoke party and DJ. Afterwards, on June 13, we'll explore the capital of the Netherlands together at walking tours and boat tours.

  • So come hang out with your fellow devs in Amsterdam! June 12 – afterparty at the same venue; June 13 – walking tours and boat tours.

In-person afterparty on June 12

JS Open Source Awards

The main goal of this project is to shed some light on great open source projects that don’t receive enough attention on a regular basis.

We’re looking at OSS projects or initiatives following an open and transparent culture, especially those eager to collaborate and receive contributions from anyone interested.

JS Open Source AWARDS
Nominations:
Breakthrough of the Year
Productivity Booster
AI Project of the Year
The Most Impactful Contribution to the Community
Submit or suggest a project

Giving back to community

We’re committed to making our events welcoming, accessible, and inclusive for a diverse global community. We offer scholarships for underrepresented and underfinanced individuals in tech, as well as collaboration opportunities for content creators – selected applicants receive a free pass. GitNation provides 100 scholarships, and we welcome partners interested in sponsoring more.

100 of 100 extra diversity scholarships sponsored
